sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
# Game over again! The level has reset with new green objects.
# Let me analyze the new layout and click all green objects.
green_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
print("Green objects:", len(green_nodes))
for n in green_nodes:
    print("  ", n['id'], n['boundary'])
agent_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] in ['Y', 'b']]
print("Agent nodes:", agent_nodes)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

If `MOUSE` is valid, use integer `row` and `col`.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
# Same level as before with 7 green objects. Agent at rows 37-40, cols 20-22.
# Click all 7 green objects.
green_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
clicks = []
for n in green_nodes:
    rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
    center_row = sum(rows) // len(rows)
    center_col = sum(cols) // len(cols)
    clicks.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': center_row, 'col': center_col})
print("Clicks:", clicks)
action(clicks)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
